external_references.title: Traefik Proxy v3.7 ForwardAuth configuration
external_references.supports: forwardBody enables buffered request-body forwarding; maxBodySize bounds it; forwarding the body breaks streaming

host_model_constraints: The selected forwardAuth service observes the authorization request before the upstream request and cannot inspect the later upstream response headers or body.

Traefik v3.7 supports buffered forwardAuth request bodies with forwardBody and maxBodySize, but the checked-in dynamic configuration does not enable them and the service config sets request_body_mode=none.
Traefik documents that forwardBody buffering breaks streaming, so streaming request-body inspection is outside this host path.
The authorization service currently sees the Traefik-to-service socket endpoints and does not map original client connection endpoints into Common connection metadata.

The full-lifecycle profile separately selects native_middleware in a pinned Traefik local-plugin host probe. Its PassthroughEngine proves plugin loading and router traffic only; it has no Common/libmodsecurity bridge and does not alter this forwardAuth compatibility declaration or promote capabilities.

connection_metadata.reason: The HTTP authorization service maps its local Traefik peer socket rather than the original client connection metadata.

request_headers.reason: Configured forwardAuth request headers are mapped into Common Runtime; canonical multi-header and limit evidence is pending.

request_body_buffered.reason: Traefik v3.7 supports forwardBody plus maxBodySize, but the checked-in middleware omits both and the connector config disables request bodies; see the versioned official reference.

request_body_streaming.reason: Traefik v3.7 documents that forwardBody reads and buffers the body before forwarding and therefore breaks streaming.

response_headers.reason: forwardAuth runs before the upstream request and does not receive the later upstream response headers.

response_body_buffered.reason: forwardAuth does not receive the later upstream response body.

response_body_streaming.reason: forwardAuth does not receive an upstream response body stream.

phase1.reason: The forwardAuth request headers enter the Common Runtime Phase-1 path; a fresh canonical baseline result is pending.

phase2.reason: The checked-in host middleware does not set forwardBody and the connector config disables request-body processing.

phase3.reason: The pre-upstream forwardAuth call cannot inspect upstream response headers.

phase4.reason: The pre-upstream forwardAuth call cannot inspect upstream response bodies.

phase4_rule_evaluation.reason: The selected Traefik forwardAuth integration runs before upstream handling and cannot inspect the later upstream response body for Phase-4 rule evaluation.

phase4_pre_commit_deny.reason: Because forwardAuth never sees the later upstream response, it has no Phase-4 response point at which to deny before the response commits.

late_intervention.reason: The authorization decision completes before the upstream response starts, so the chosen path has no response-phase late intervention point.

late_intervention_log_only.reason: The selected pre-upstream forwardAuth host model has no committed upstream response on which to apply a late log-only intervention.

late_intervention_abort.reason: The selected pre-upstream forwardAuth host model has no upstream response-phase point from which the authorization service can perform a late connection abort.

late_intervention_status_metadata.reason: The selected forwardAuth integration does not observe an upstream response, so it cannot produce Phase-4 metadata that distinguishes original and visible upstream-response status or late actions.

deny.reason: A disruptive Common Runtime decision is returned as a non-2xx forwardAuth response; a fresh canonical deny result is pending.

redirect.reason: The Common Runtime preserves redirect status and URL, but the repository HTTP authorization response does not emit the Location header for Traefik to propagate.

drop.reason: The external HTTP authorization contract returns a response and cannot directly silent-drop Traefik's client connection.

abort_connection.reason: The external authorization service cannot directly abort Traefik's client connection as a ModSecurity action.

log_only.reason: A non-disruptive rule leaves the forwardAuth response successful; explicit canonical log-only evidence is pending.

transaction_id.reason: The configured x-request-id header is resolved by Common Runtime and included in decision events; canonical correlation evidence is pending.

event_jsonl.reason: Common Runtime writes bounded metadata-only disruptive-decision JSONL; canonical field and payload-absence evidence is pending.

config_inline_rules.reason: The repository authorization-service config parser and Common Runtime support inline rules; canonical config evidence is pending.

config_rules_file.reason: The checked-in service path loads a local rules file through Common Runtime; canonical positive and negative evidence is pending.

config_remote_rules.reason: Common Runtime exposes remote rule key and URL configuration, but the no-CRS baseline does not exercise external networking.

transport_metadata.reason: The pre-upstream forwardAuth service does not observe client-visible upstream-response commitment or transport outcomes.

request_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The selected forwardAuth path disables request bodies, and Traefik forwardBody is buffered rather than incremental when enabled.

response_body_incremental_ingest.reason: The pre-upstream forwardAuth service never receives an upstream response-body stream.

phase4_end_of_stream_evaluation.reason: forwardAuth never receives the later upstream response stream or its end-of-stream event.

content_type_scope.reason: The selected forwardAuth path does not receive an upstream response Content-Type or response body to scope.

header_limits.reason: Forwarded request headers have no connector-local canonical header-limit enforcement contract.

request_body_limits.reason: The checked-in forwardAuth middleware disables request-body forwarding and defines no active request-body limit behavior.

response_body_limits.reason: forwardAuth does not receive an upstream response body on which to enforce a response-body limit.

no_full_response_buffering.reason: The request-only forwardAuth host model has no upstream response stream for this property to describe.

first_byte_before_response_end.reason: The request-only forwardAuth service cannot observe a Traefik client first byte or upstream response end.

http1_content_length.reason: Traefik can proxy HTTP/1.1 responses, but no canonical forwardAuth transport case covers Content-Length handling.

http1_chunked.reason: Traefik can proxy chunked HTTP/1.1 responses, but no canonical forwardAuth transport case covers them.

keep_alive.reason: No canonical sequential-request keep-alive run is attached to the Traefik forwardAuth path.

parallel_requests.reason: The Traefik forwardAuth full-lifecycle catalog has no parallel-request evidence path.

http2.reason: Traefik HTTP/2 transport behavior is not exercised by a canonical forwardAuth full-lifecycle case.

client_abort.reason: The external authorization service cannot observe or directly control a Traefik client-abort lifecycle.

upstream_abort.reason: The pre-upstream authorization service cannot observe or control an upstream response-abort lifecycle.

response_body_decompression.reason: forwardAuth does not receive an upstream response body to decompress.

source_contract.reason: Service, mapper, metadata, Traefik configuration, and harness sources are present; no canonical source-contract result is attached.

compile.reason: A repository authorization-service build path exists; no current canonical compile result is attached.

link.reason: The service links Common Runtime and libmodsecurity; no current canonical link result is attached.

config_load.reason: Connector configuration and Traefik file-provider checks exist; no current canonical result is attached.

start_smoke.reason: Traefik and forwardAuth service request-free start wiring exists; no current canonical result is attached.

minimal_runtime_smoke.reason: A real Traefik forwardAuth 200/403 harness exists; no current canonical minimal-runtime result is attached.

no_crs_baseline.reason: Capability-selected pre-upstream cases can run through the host path, but no canonical baseline result is attached.

crs_smoke.reason: Legacy limited CRS smoke wiring exists and is not baseline evidence.

extended_matrix.reason: Extended-matrix verification is outside this canonical no-CRS declaration.
